Cc: contracts@heliohealth.com
Date: Mon, 12 Jul 2027 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: DPA redlines: runtime-access carveout + list of subprocessors

Hi Ben —

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our legal team attached a marked-up DPA (see helio_dpa_redlines_v3.pdf) with two items they consider blockers for our security review:

1) An explicit runtime-access carveout that would allow Helio to request transient runtime KV cache dumps for debugging in our environment (we'd like to limit this to a small set of sessions and require on-demand approvals).
2) A requirement to approve a minimal list of subprocessors that will handle inference runtime telemetry; the wording in Section 7.2 is currently too broad for our procurement.

Can you route this to your product/security leads and confirm what acceptance path you have...

Date: Tue, 6 Jul 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: rw-storm-6b: elevated HTTP errors & short-term mitigation

Monica / Aditya,

We are seeing an uptick in 5xx and timeout responses for rw-storm-6b starting ~08:50 PDT. E...
text:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 6 Jul 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: rw-storm-6b: elevated HTTP errors & short-term mitigation

Monica / Aditya,

We are seeing an uptick in 5xx and timeout responses for rw-storm-6b starting ~08:50 PDT. Error spike appears concentrated on longer-context generation jobs (>= 1024 tokens) and on a handful of BrightBank customers routing to the model. Initial signs point to memory backpressure on a subset of GPU hosts when KV cache grows quickly.

What I've done so far:
- Scaled up instance pool for rw-storm-6b by +20% (warm nodes coming up).
- Flipped aggressive prefix cache eviction to free KV memory on the busiest hosts.
- Notified BrightBank ops; holding a sync at 10:00 to share temporary mitigations.

Immediate ask:
- Aditya: can you validate kernel OOM/restart logs on hos...

Ben — attaching our redline (ACME_MSA_Redline_v3.docx) and a short summary. Two ask areas that legal flagged: 

1) Auto-renew: we want language that makes renewal automatic for subsequent 12-month terms unless we opt-out with 60 days' written notice (current draft is 30 days).

2) Termination for convenience (T4C): we prefer T4C to be allowed at the Order Form level only (so Acme can terminate a specific service/order) and limited to a 30-day transition period. We are willing to accept a pro-rated refund for prepaid amounts.

Hi Ben —

Thanks for the quick intro last week. VectorOps built a lightweight sync layer that keeps a managed vector index (e.g., Pinecone)...
text:
From: Elena Ruiz <elena@vectorops.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Sync model & TTL approach for Pinecone <> S3 pilot

Hi Ben —

Thanks for the quick intro last week. VectorOps built a lightweight sync layer that keeps a managed vector index (e.g., Pinecone) warm while using S3 as the canonical cold store. We have a few open questions on TTL semantics and consistency for a pilot with Redwood:

- Where should TTL enforcement live (index vs. cold store vs. service layer)?
- How strict do you need read-after-write guarantees for fresh embeddings?
- Is cross-region replication required for the pilot, or can we scope it to a single region?

Attached a short proposal (vectorops-ttl-proposal.pdf) that lays out two models: write-through (sync to index then async to S3) and write-to-canonical (S3 first, index warmed lazily).

We’d love...

Subject: Request: segmented remit + monthly bridge for enterprise pilot

Hi Alex,

Thanks again for the demo last week — SVB Beacon is excited to move into a paid pilot for Q3. A couple of billing constraints from our finance team before we finalize:

- We need invoices split by legal entity (US operations vs EU subsidiary) for tax and local payment routing.
- For Q3 only, we’d like to remain on quarterly billing cadence, but we need a short-term monthly \"bridge\" invoice for the first two months while our internal accounting cutovers finish.
- Payment terms: we can agree to Net-90 on the quarterly invoices, provided you can issue the monthly bridge invoices with Net-30.

Can Redwood do segmented remit (two invoices per quarter) plus the two monthly bridge invoices? If so...
